title: IISc Administrative Assistant Guess Paper 5 (Surprise Paper)
subtitle: Boundary-testing paper covering 2026 syllabus topics skipped in 2023

metadata.strategy: Deliberately includes syllabus topics the 2023 examiner skipped: averages, trigonometry, heights and distances, data interpretation, surds, Venn diagrams, statement-conclusion, error spotting, voice, narration, IT Act, digital signatures and e-governance.

instructions: Total 80 questions to be attempted in 90 minutes.
Negative marking applies: 0.33333 marks (one-third) are deducted for every incorrect answer.
Figure-based placeholder questions are unscored and carry 0 marks either way.
This surprise paper stretches to the edges of the official syllabus; expect topics not seen in the 2023 paper.

questions.text: The average of 5 numbers is 27. When one number is removed, the average of the remaining numbers becomes 25. Enter the removed number.
A batsman averages 32 runs in 10 innings. He scores 76 in the 11th innings. His new average is:
If tan(theta) = 3/4 and theta is acute, then sin(theta) equals:
questions.explanation: In a 3-4-5 triangle, sin = 3/5.

questions.text: The value of sin 30 degrees + cos 60 degrees is:
From a point 30 m away from the foot of a tower, the angle of elevation of its top is 60 degrees. The height of the tower is:
questions.explanation: Height = 30 x tan 60 = 30 sqrt(3).

questions.text: A pie chart of a monthly budget of Rs 36000 shows: Food 120 degrees, Rent 90 degrees, Travel 60 degrees, Savings 90 degrees. The amount spent on Rent is:
In the same budget pie chart (total Rs 36000; Food 120 degrees, Travel 60 degrees), spending on Food exceeds spending on Travel by:
questions.explanation: The 60-degree gap equals (60/360) x 36000 = 6000.

questions.text: The value of the square root of 0.0081 is:
After rationalising the denominator, 1/(sqrt(5) - 2) equals:
questions.explanation: Multiply by (sqrt(5)+2); denominator becomes 5 - 4 = 1.

questions.text: Simplify: sqrt(48) + sqrt(27)
questions.explanation: 4 sqrt(3) + 3 sqrt(3) = 7 sqrt(3).

questions.text: A number is decreased by 10% and the result is again decreased by 10%. The overall decrease is:
A trader sells two articles at Rs 990 each, gaining 10% on one and losing 10% on the other. Overall he makes:
questions.explanation: CPs are 900 and 1100; SP 1980 vs CP 2000 is a 1% loss.

questions.text: A car covers 150 km at 50 km/h and the next 150 km at 75 km/h. Its average speed for the whole journey is:
questions.explanation: 300 km in 3 + 2 = 5 hours gives 60 km/h.

questions.text: The compound interest on Rs 5000 for 1 year at 20% per annum, compounded half-yearly, is:
Each interior angle of a regular octagon measures:
A right prism has a square base of side 5 cm and height 12 cm. Its volume is:
Spot the part with the error: (a) One of the students / (b) have failed / (c) in the examination. / (d) No error
questions.explanation: 'One of the students' takes the singular 'has failed'.

questions.text: Spot the part with the error: (a) The committee have / (b) submitted its report / (c) to the director. / (d) No error
questions.explanation: Acting as one body, 'the committee has' is correct.

questions.text: He is addicted ____ gambling.
questions.explanation: 'Addicted' takes the preposition 'to'.

questions.text: The committee is composed ____ ten members.
questions.explanation: 'Composed of' is the correct phrase.

questions.text: Change to passive voice: 'Someone stole my purse.'
questions.explanation: Simple past active becomes was + past participle.

questions.text: Change to indirect speech: He said, 'I am busy.'

options.text: He said that he is busy.
He said that he was busy.
He says that he was busy.
He told that he is busy.

questions.explanation: Present tense shifts to past in reported speech.

questions.text: Rearrange the parts to form a meaningful sentence: P: by the heavy rains / Q: the old bridge / R: was washed away / S: last monsoon
questions.explanation: The old bridge was washed away by the heavy rains last monsoon.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: The principal DISCUSSED ABOUT the new timetable.
questions.explanation: 'Discuss' never takes 'about'.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To turn a deaf ear
questions.explanation: It means to refuse to listen.

questions.text: One who is present everywhere:
questions.explanation: Omnipresent means present everywhere.

questions.text: Select the synonym of BENEVOLENT:
questions.explanation: Benevolent means kind and charitable.

questions.text: Select the antonym of OPTIMIST:
questions.explanation: A pessimist expects the worst.

questions.text: Select the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: Double n with -aire: questionnaire.

questions.passage: Governments around the world are experimenting with artificial intelligence to deliver public services. Chatbots answer citizens' queries about pensions, algorithms flag suspicious tax returns, and translation tools make official portals available in dozens of languages. Supporters argue that AI cuts queues and paperwork, freeing officials for complex cases. Critics counter that automated decisions can be opaque and may quietly discriminate against the very citizens they are meant to serve. Most experts therefore recommend keeping a human officer responsible for every final decision, using AI as an assistant rather than a judge.
questions.text: According to the passage, critics of AI in public services fear that automated decisions may be:
questions.explanation: The passage cites opacity and quiet discrimination.

questions.passage: Governments around the world are experimenting with artificial intelligence to deliver public services. Chatbots answer citizens' queries about pensions, algorithms flag suspicious tax returns, and translation tools make official portals available in dozens of languages. Supporters argue that AI cuts queues and paperwork, freeing officials for complex cases. Critics counter that automated decisions can be opaque and may quietly discriminate against the very citizens they are meant to serve. Most experts therefore recommend keeping a human officer responsible for every final decision, using AI as an assistant rather than a judge.
questions.text: What do most experts recommend in the passage?

options.text: Letting AI take all routine decisions
Keeping a human responsible for final decisions

questions.explanation: AI should assist, with humans making final decisions.

questions.passage: Governments around the world are experimenting with artificial intelligence to deliver public services. Chatbots answer citizens' queries about pensions, algorithms flag suspicious tax returns, and translation tools make official portals available in dozens of languages. Supporters argue that AI cuts queues and paperwork, freeing officials for complex cases. Critics counter that automated decisions can be opaque and may quietly discriminate against the very citizens they are meant to serve. Most experts therefore recommend keeping a human officer responsible for every final decision, using AI as an assistant rather than a judge.
questions.text: In the passage, the word 'opaque' most nearly means:
questions.explanation: Opaque decisions cannot be seen through or explained.

questions.text: Find the next term: 2, 12, 36, 80, 150, ?
Find the next term: 13, 17, 19, 23, ?
questions.explanation: These are consecutive primes.

questions.text: Find the next term: Z, X, U, Q, L, ?
questions.explanation: Gaps grow: -2, -3, -4, -5, then -6.

questions.text: If STAR is coded as RATS, how is LAMP coded?
questions.explanation: The word is simply reversed.

questions.text: If DOG = 52, then CAT = ?
questions.explanation: Twice the sum of positions: 2 x (3+1+20) = 48.

questions.text: Which word CANNOT be formed from the letters of CREATION?
questions.explanation: CREATION has no letter M.

questions.text: Which diagram best represents the relationship between Furniture, Chairs and Tables?

options.text: Two non-overlapping circles inside a larger circle
Two overlapping circles inside a larger circle

questions.explanation: Chairs and tables are distinct kinds of furniture.

questions.text: In a class of 60 students, 35 like tea, 30 like coffee and 15 like both. How many like neither?
If @ means 'multiplied by' and # means 'added to', then 2 # 3 @ 4 = ?
Select the option that EXACTLY matches: Flat 4B, 221 M.G. Road, Bengaluru-560001
questions.explanation: The others alter the number, PIN or flat.

questions.text: V, W, X, Y, Z sit in a row facing north. X sits exactly in the middle. V is to the immediate left of X. Z is at the extreme right. W is between X and Z. Who is at the extreme left?
questions.explanation: Order is Y, V, X, W, Z.

questions.text: Sessions on Maths, Physics, Chemistry and Biology run Monday to Thursday, one per day. Biology is on Monday. Chemistry is immediately after Biology. Maths is on Thursday. On which day is Physics?
questions.explanation: Bio Mon, Chem Tue, Maths Thu leaves Physics on Wednesday.

questions.text: A is B's sister. C is B's mother. D is C's father. How is A related to D?
questions.explanation: A is the child of C, whose father is D.

questions.text: Riya walks 6 km west, turns right and walks 8 km. How far is she from her starting point?
Statement: All the windows of the new office are made of unbreakable glass. Conclusions: I. No window of the office can be broken easily. II. The office authorities feared theft.
questions.explanation: I restates the fact; II assumes an unstated motive.

questions.text: Statement: A notice in the library reads, 'Use of mobile phones is prohibited inside the library.' Conclusions: I. Some visitors bring mobile phones to the library. II. Mobile phones always disturb readers.
questions.explanation: The notice implies phones are brought in; II is too absolute.

questions.text: During 2025, electric-vehicle registrations in a city doubled while petrol-vehicle registrations fell by 10%. Which conclusion best follows?

options.text: Petrol will be banned next year
Electric vehicles are gaining acceptance in the city
Electric vehicles are cheaper than petrol vehicles

questions.explanation: The trend supports growing acceptance; the rest overreach.

questions.text: Statements: All keys are locks. All locks are doors. Some doors are windows. Conclusions: I. Some doors are keys. II. Some windows are locks.
questions.explanation: Keys are doors, so some doors are keys; II is uncertain.

questions.text: On a standard die numbered 1 to 6, the number 2 is adjacent to 1, 3, 4 and 6. Which number is opposite 2?
questions.explanation: The only remaining face, 5, must be opposite.

questions.text: A square sheet is folded twice and a hole is punched as shown. Select how the sheet appears when unfolded.

figure.label: Punched Hole Pattern
figure.description: A paper folded twice with one punch; the unfolded hole pattern must be chosen from four options.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual punching question.

questions.text: Select the option in which the given simple figure is embedded.

figure.label: Embedded Figure
figure.description: A simple geometric shape that must be located inside one of four complex figures.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual embedded-figure question.

questions.text: The first pair of figures shares a relationship. Select the figure that completes the second pair in the same way.

figure.label: Figural Analogy
figure.description: Figure A relates to figure B by rotation and shading; the option matching C to D in the same way must be chosen.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual analogy question.

questions.text: Ice floats on water because:

options.text: Its density is lower than that of water

questions.explanation: Ice is less dense, so it floats.

questions.text: Which vitamin is produced in human skin on exposure to sunlight?
questions.explanation: Sunlight triggers vitamin D synthesis in the skin.

questions.text: CRISPR technology, frequently in the news, is used for:
questions.explanation: CRISPR allows precise editing of DNA.

questions.text: The Chief Justice of India is appointed by:
questions.explanation: The President formally appoints the CJI.

questions.text: The words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' were added to the Preamble by which Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added them.

questions.text: The Kalinga War, after which Ashoka embraced Buddhism, was fought around:
questions.explanation: The war took place about 261 BCE.

questions.text: Sattriya, a classical dance recognised by the Sangeet Natak Akademi, belongs to:
questions.explanation: Sattriya originated in the monasteries of Assam.

questions.text: The Ten Degree Channel separates:

options.text: The Andaman and Nicobar island groups

questions.explanation: It runs between the Andamans and the Nicobars.

questions.text: Retail inflation in India is primarily measured by:
questions.explanation: CPI tracks retail prices for consumers.

questions.text: SIDBI is a financial institution that primarily supports:
questions.explanation: SIDBI is the Small Industries Development Bank of India.

questions.text: The COP29 climate summit (November 2024) was held in:
questions.explanation: Azerbaijan hosted COP29 in Baku.

questions.text: The Khelo India programme is aimed at:

options.text: Building sports culture and grassroots talent

questions.explanation: It develops grassroots sports across India.

questions.text: The currency of Bangladesh is the:
questions.explanation: Bangladesh uses the taka.

questions.text: The Digital India programme was launched in:
questions.explanation: It was launched on 1 July 2015.

questions.text: 'Exam Warriors' is a book written by:
questions.explanation: PM Modi wrote it for students facing exams.

questions.text: The ancient university of Nalanda, whose modern campus was inaugurated in 2024, is located in:
questions.explanation: Nalanda is in Bihar, near Rajgir.

questions.text: The Information Technology Act of India was enacted in the year:
questions.explanation: The IT Act came into force in 2000.

questions.text: Under the IT Act, Section 66 broadly deals with:
questions.explanation: Section 66 covers dishonest or fraudulent computer offences.

questions.text: A digital signature primarily ensures:

options.text: Authentication and integrity of a document

questions.explanation: It verifies the signer and detects tampering.

questions.text: Filing income-tax returns on the government portal is an example of which e-Governance interaction?
questions.explanation: The government provides a service directly to citizens.

questions.text: DigiLocker is a government platform used for:
questions.explanation: DigiLocker holds digital copies of official documents.

questions.text: An information kiosk is best described as:

options.text: A public terminal providing access to information and services

questions.explanation: Kiosks are self-service public information terminals.

questions.text: NTFS is:

options.text: A file system used by Windows

questions.explanation: NTFS is the New Technology File System of Windows.

questions.text: Which of the following is an input device?
questions.explanation: An OMR reader feeds marked data into the computer.

questions.text: In Excel, what does the formula =LEN("IISC") return?
questions.explanation: LEN counts the four characters.

questions.text: In MS PowerPoint, pressing F5 will:

options.text: Start the slide show from the beginning

questions.explanation: F5 starts the slide show from slide 1.
